revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24755395338|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:伊米神社八幡宮]] {{Expand Japanese|伊米神社八幡宮}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ime Shrine & Hachimangu | native_name = 伊米神社八幡宮 | image = Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine (2), Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ime Shrine & Hachimangu | coordinates = {{coord|37.31583305555556|138.7825}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|zh|天香山命|qid=Q10940849|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ime Shrine & Hachimangu'''|伊米神社八幡宮}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135038714|ltq=Q135038714|qid=Q135038714|1=Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q11390939|ltq=Q11390939|qid=Q11390939|1=Hachiman shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinai Supershrine|lt=Shikinai Supershrine|qid=Q135419779|1=Shikinai Supershrine}} * {{ill|Q29431432|ltq=Q29431432|qid=Q29431432|1=dyad}} == part of (P361) == * ** P156: {{ill|Q11478109|ltq=Q11478109|qid=Q11478109|1=Kawai Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q819174|ltq=Q819174|qid=Q819174|1=Ojiya, Niigata}} * d'Echigo|gl|Provincia de Echigo|id|Provinsi Echigo|it|Echigo|ja|越後国|ko|에치고국|lt|Ečigo žemė|nl|Echigo (provincie)|pt|Província de Echigo|ru|Этиго (провинция)|simple|Echigo Province|sk|Ečigo (provincia)|sr|Провинција Ечиго|tr|Echigo (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Етіґо|zh_min_nan|Etigo-kok|zh_yue|越後國|zh|越後國|qid=Q666514|1=Echigo Province}} * {{ill|Q11674002|ltq=Q11674002|qid=Q11674002|1=Uonuma district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|37.31583305555556|138.7825}} == image (P18) == * Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine (2), Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q10940849|ltq=Q10940849|qid=Q10940849|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いめじんじゃはちまんぐう == postal code (P281) == * 947-0035https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2110005012812https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Imai Shrine|lt=Imai Shrine|qid=Q135195099|1=Imai Shrine}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P1352: {'amount': '+1', 'unit': '1'} ** P3831: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135195100|ltq=Q135195100|qid=Q135195100|1=Shimizu‑Kawabe Shrine}} ** P1352: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} ** P3831: {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa Shrine|qid=Q135195101|1=Suwa Shrine}} ** P1352: {'amount': '+3', 'unit': '1'} ** P3831: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135195102|ltq=Q135195102|qid=Q135195102|1=Ichinomiya Shrine}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P1352: {'amount': '+4', 'unit': '1'} ** P3831: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135195103|ltq=Q135195103|qid=Q135195103|1=Imai Shrine}} ** P1352: {'amount': '+5', 'unit': '1'} ** P3831: {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '新潟県小千谷市桜町1314', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '新潟県小千谷市大字桜町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:伊米神社八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11379707}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ime Shrine & Hachimangu |画像= [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine (2), Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|270px|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]]
Haiden and temizuya |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|qid=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|qid=Q819174|1=Ojiya, Niigata}}Sakuramachi 1314 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} disputed identification |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Ime Shrine
Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine, Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii]] '''Ime Shrine & Hachimangu''' (いめじんじゃはちまんぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sakuramachi, {{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|qid=Q819174|1=Ojiya, Niigata}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|qid=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for "Ime Shrine," recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as one of the five seats in {{ill|lt=Uonuma District|Q11674002|qid=Q11674002|1=Uonuma district}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|qid=Q10940849|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} * Betsumitama Ime-Ōkami (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * Tsushima-hime-no-Mikoto (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|qid=Q6080112|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (in ''Onko no Shiori'') * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (co-enshrined) === History === Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was ordered to pacify {{ill|lt=Koshi Province|Q1351611|qid=Q1351611|1=Koshi Province (Japan)}} and arrived in this area. He first lived in Kami-Ime (Ryōshinden, now in Ojiya), later in Shimo-Ime (Sakuramachi), and then in the area called Imegahara, where present-day Ime Hachimangu stands. ''Ime Hachimangu Koshōsho-jō'' records: "Ime-Ōkami is Imehiko-no-Mikoto and settled here; therefore this place is called Imehara. He had one son and one daughter. The elder was named Kamiine Nagaho, and the younger princess was called Imetara-hime... The place where Ime Nagaho-ō sat is now called Kami-Ime, and the place where Imetara-hime sat is called Shimo-Ime." It is also said that while he lived here he was called Tegurihiko-no-Mikoto. When Minamoto no Yoshiie passed through this area, he fought the local Emishi. His retainer Nagano hid Yoshiie beneath Tōguni Bridge and saved his life. Yoshiie enshrined the protective principal image from inside his helmet as Hachimangu and allowed the Nagano family to govern the area. Another account says that in {{ill|lt=1064|Q19404|qid=Q19404|1=1064}}, when Yoshiie returned from Ōshū to Kyoto, he gave thanks for victory and left two retainers (Nagano and Abe, or Nakamachi and Nagano) to guard Hachiman Shrine. After that, Hachimangu was enshrined together with Ime Shrine. === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|qid=Q10935105|lt=Sannō Daigongen|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} (Ryūagaru, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Toyoki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|qid=Q11633375|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} (Shinbo, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|qid=Q461258|lt=Kusanagi Daigongen|1=Yamato Takeru}} (Kusanagi, Ichinomiya village) * Haguro Daigongen (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin|1=Takeminakata}} (Aburashinden, Ichinomiya village) * Yamamiya Suwa (Miyayama, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Yamada, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Kurainetama Shrine|1=Ukanomitama}} (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Amida-dō|Q11657414|qid=Q11657414|1=Amida-dō}} (formerly at the site of present Ojiya Nishi High School) ** Ichinomiya village corresponds to the present Sakuramachi area. === Kami-Ime Springs === There are two springs: Kami-Ime Spring and Shimo-Ime Spring. * It is said that drinking while praying to the deity of Ime drives away evil influences and disasters. * It is said that Kami-Ime Spring helps the left eye, and Shimo-Ime Spring helps the right eye. * It is said that men are healed by drinking Kami-Ime Spring, and women by drinking Shimo-Ime Spring. * A legend says that when the wife of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira|Q2994735|qid=Q2994735|1=Fujiwara no Tokihira}} and {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kanehira|Q106234851|qid=Q106234851|1=Fujiwara no Kanehira}} were headed to Yoshidani in Ojiya and had no water, Kanehira faced toward Kyoto and prayed to {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}: "Please forgive the sins of the Fujiwara clan and grant us water." Water then welled up. This came to be called "Water of Deep Emotion," though it is also said the story arose because "Kami-Ime" and "kanmei" sound similar. === Shrine buildings === * Honden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** South-facing round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 1 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 1 ken 1 shaku * Haiden ** Round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 4 ken ** Depth: 4 ken * Heiden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 2 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 2.5 ken === References === {{参照方法|date=2020-03|section=1}} * ''Ime Jinja Shashi'' (Ime Shrine history), Ime Shrine History Editorial Committee. * Igarashi Hidetarō, ''Legends of Ojiya'', Kōbunsha. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:History of Ojiya]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ojiya, Niigata]]
